---
title: FreeCampus Python
description: A beginner-first Python course from zero programming experience to practical projects.
---

::: {.hero}
# Python from Zero to Hero

This course is for learners who are new to programming. It starts in Google
Colab, then gradually introduces local Python, VS Code, Jupyter, debugging,
testing, scientific computing, data science, introductory AI libraries, and a
capstone portfolio project.
:::

## Start here

- Read the [lesson index](lessons/index.qmd) to understand the full path.
- Use [learner profiles](lessons/learner-profiles.qmd) to choose a study path.
- See [instructor notes](lessons/instructor-notes.qmd) for teaching guidance.
- Begin with [What Is Programming?](lessons/getting-started/what-is-programming.qmd)
  if programming is new to you.
- Use [Google Colab](lessons/getting-started/google-colab.qmd) if you want to
  practice immediately without installing anything.
- Study [Lists](lessons/data-structures/lists.qmd) when you are ready to work
  with ordered collections.
- Keep [Error Messages](lessons/debugging/error-messages.qmd) nearby whenever
  Python prints red text.

## Course promise

The course teaches programming as a learnable process:

1. predict what code will do;
2. run it;
3. answer the section quiz;
4. explain the result in everyday language;
5. change one small thing;
6. debug using clues instead of guessing.

## Tools used

- [Google Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/) for no-install practice.
- [Python](https://www.python.org/downloads/) for local programming.
- [VS Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/) for project work.
- [Jupyter](https://jupyter.org/) for notebooks and data exploration.
- `ruff`, `pytest`, type hints, and CI for code quality.
- NumPy, pandas, Matplotlib, scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow/Keras, and
  Transformers for applied work.

## Course chapters

- [Getting Started](lessons/getting-started/index.qmd) — Set up your learning environment and understand how Python runs before memorizing syntax.
- [Core Python](lessons/core-python/index.qmd) — Learn the small building blocks that almost every Python program uses: values, names, decisions, and repetition.
- [Data Structures](lessons/data-structures/index.qmd) — Store, organize, search, and transform many values with the right container for the job.
- [Functions](lessons/functions/index.qmd) — Package useful steps behind names so code can be reused, tested, and explained.
- [Debugging](lessons/debugging/index.qmd) — Read errors calmly, inspect state, and make small experiments instead of guessing.
- [Files and Data](lessons/files-and-data/index.qmd) — Read, write, validate, and recover from problems when programs interact with data outside memory.
- [Projects and Environments](lessons/projects-and-environments/index.qmd) — Organize code into files, install dependencies safely, and run programs as small projects.
- [Code Quality](lessons/code-quality/index.qmd) — Make code trustworthy with tests, style, formatting, linting, types, and automation.
- [Object-Oriented Python](lessons/object-oriented-python/index.qmd) — Use classes when data and behavior belong together, while avoiding unnecessary complexity.
- [Advanced Python Patterns](lessons/advanced-python-patterns/index.qmd) — Learn powerful Python patterns one at a time after core concepts are stable.
- [Scientific Python](lessons/scientific-python/index.qmd) — Use Python's scientific stack for arrays, simulation, plotting, symbolic math, and numerical methods.
- [Data Science](lessons/data-science/index.qmd) — Turn questions and datasets into cleaned, analyzed, visualized, and communicated results.
- [Machine Learning and AI](lessons/machine-learning-ai/index.qmd) — Understand AI vocabulary, train simple models, evaluate them responsibly, and know where major libraries fit.
- [Capstone](lessons/capstone/index.qmd) — Plan, build, test, document, and present a beginner-friendly Python project that integrates the course.
